title: Vendored map data, projections, and topology-safe simplification
short_description: Vendor Natural Earth (1:110m, 1:50m) and historical-basemaps as offline version-pinned package data with a territory index (id to geometry plus date validity) under the wheel-size policy, and implement pinned-pyproj projections (equal-earth default, azimuthal regional) with topology-aware simplification proven gap/overlap-free on adjacent-country fixtures.
long_description_md:
# Vendored map data, projections, and topology-safe simplification

## Why this task exists

This task establishes the geometry foundation for **Phase 4: Map engine**, covering the phase's first two completion requirements:

- Vendored Natural Earth 1:110m and 1:50m data plus historical-basemaps, available offline through a territory index with date validity and packaged under the wheel-size policy.
- Pinned-pyproj world and regional projections plus topology-aware simplification proven not to introduce gaps or overlaps between adjacent territories.

Maps are a v1 core primitive and one of anima-ai's most defensible differentiators. The feasibility research found an unoccupied library niche for code-authored history and geopolitics maps, while the product and architecture specs make deterministic semantic verification the central promise. Later map nodes, verification rules, and rendering code must therefore resolve the same frozen territory geometry every time; mutable upstream data, runtime downloads, projection-axis ambiguity, or independently simplified borders would invalidate that promise.

The resulting modules are the data and geometry substrate consumed later by `BaseMap`, `Territory`, `Border`, `InvasionArrow`, map cameras, `anima assets --territories`, and `MAP.*` verification rules. This task does not implement those consumers.

## What this task builds

### 1. Reproducible vendored datasets

Vendor pre-converted GeoJSON for both required Natural Earth scales and a vetted historical-basemaps corpus. Every bundled dataset must have an immutable dataset ID and version plus a checked-in provenance manifest recording its upstream repository or release, immutable revision, source-file checksums, conversion-schema version, coordinate reference system, license, and attribution requirements.

Normalize the release data ahead of packaging rather than at runtime. Packaged GeoJSON must use WGS84 longitude/latitude coordinates, deterministic feature and property ordering, documented coordinate precision, valid polygonal geometry, and explicit canonical territory IDs. Natural Earth IDs must be curated where upstream identifier fields are missing, duplicated, or unstable, with one deterministic override table shared by the 1:110m and 1:50m variants. Historical source names or IDs must remain available as provenance or aliases without becoming unstable public IDs.

Natural Earth's public-domain notice and the historical dataset's vetted license and attribution must ship inside package data. Historical files must not be bundled until their redistribution terms are recorded and compatible with the project's licensing requirements. Any maintainer-only refresh utility may acquire or transform upstream data explicitly, but released library paths must never download, update, or phone home.

### 2. Wheel-safe offline resource loading

Expose dataset manifests and files through `importlib.resources` so loading works from an installed wheel without relying on the repository checkout, current working directory, or unpacked filesystem paths. The loader must use the existing socket guard successfully and must not invoke geopandas, GDAL, pyogrio, a shell, or any runtime downloader; GeoJSON parsing uses stdlib `json` plus Shapely.

Build and inspect the actual compressed distribution artifacts. If all required data keeps the `anima-ai` wheel at or below the architecture's approximately 60 MiB policy, ship it in the main wheel. If it exceeds that limit, create the `anima-ai-data` companion wheel, keep the main wheel below the limit, pin compatible package/data versions, and make the resource loader behave identically once both wheels are installed. A missing or incompatible data wheel must raise a typed, actionable package-data error rather than attempting a download.

### 3. Territory index with temporal validity

Implement the `MapDataset` foundation described in `data-model.md`: a registry of dataset ID, version, license metadata, and territory records. Each record maps a dataset-scoped canonical territory ID to its geometry and metadata, including an optional validity interval. Use half-open date intervals, `[valid_from, valid_to)`, with `null` representing an unbounded endpoint; current Natural Earth records may be unbounded, while historical records must carry the source-supported interval.

The index must support deterministic dataset enumeration, territory enumeration, direct ID lookup, alias lookup where curated, and resolution for a `datetime.date`. Date-based resolution must return exactly one geometry or a typed not-found/ambiguous result; it must never silently choose among overlapping historical records. Validate at build or test time that IDs are unique within a dataset version, validity intervals for the same canonical identity do not overlap unintentionally, referenced geometries exist and are valid, and serialized index output is stable across runs. Geometry should be referenced once rather than duplicated into the index solely for lookup convenience.

### 4. Deterministic projection layer

Implement a focused projection module over pinned pyproj. The world-map default is Equal Earth using EPSG:8857. The regional default is a WGS84 Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection centered on an explicit longitude and latitude supplied by the caller. Always interpret geographic input as `(longitude, latitude)` by constructing transformers with `always_xy=True`.

Projection definitions must be canonical and serializable so future scene inspection and render metadata can identify the exact projection recipe. Cache transformers only by canonical immutable projection parameters, disable PROJ network access, and expose the pyproj and underlying PROJ versions used. Forward and inverse transformation must support points, lines, polygons, multipolygons, and holes without changing geometry type or dropping components. Tests must cover representative world coordinates, regional centers and edges, antimeridian-adjacent multipolygons, invalid parameters, deterministic repeat calls, and forward/inverse round trips within documented floating-point tolerance.

### 5. Shared-topology simplification

Implement simplification over a complete related feature collection, not independently per polygon. Construct shared arcs with topojson, apply explicit quantization and simplification parameters once to each shared arc, and reconstruct Shapely geometries from the same simplified coordinates on both sides of every border. Parameters and coordinate-space assumptions must be public, deterministic, and serializable. Invalid tolerance or quantization values, topology construction failures, and simplification that collapses a required territory must fail explicitly rather than returning damaged geometry.

Prove the topology guarantee with synthetic and vendored adjacent-country fixtures at multiple simplification tolerances. The fixtures must include a nontrivial shared border and coverage mask, with multipart or holed geometry represented. For every tested tolerance, assert that outputs are valid and nonempty, expected neighbors retain one coincident shared boundary, pairwise interior overlap area is at most `1e-10` of fixture coverage area, and uncovered area inside the expected simplified coverage mask is at most the same relative tolerance. Repeat simplification must produce identical canonical geometry bytes or hashes. Include a regression demonstrating that naive per-polygon simplification can fail the fixture while the shared-topology implementation passes it.

## Explicitly out of scope

- Map scene-node classes, Skia drawing, style-specific map goldens, arrows, markers, or labels.
- Map camera movement, viewport flights, or cross-scene map-state persistence.
- `MAP.UNKNOWN_TERRITORY`, `MAP.DATE_MISMATCH`, projection-defined, or arrow-in-view verification findings.
- `anima assets --territories`; this task supplies the index that command will later consume.
- Historical border interpolation or border-morph animation between dates.
- Runtime acquisition of optional map data or a geopandas/GDAL dependency.

## Acceptance shape

Done means wheels built from a clean checkout can be installed and exercised without repository access or network access; every required dataset can be enumerated and representative current and historical territories resolve to valid geometry at the correct dates; provenance, licenses, versions, and checksums are shipped; the main-wheel or companion-wheel outcome satisfies the approximately 60 MiB policy; Equal Earth and centered regional azimuthal projections round-trip deterministically under the pinned pyproj/PROJ stack; and adjacent-country fixtures remain gap-free and overlap-free after topology-aware simplification.

deliverables.description: Version-pinned Natural Earth 1:110m and 1:50m plus historical-basemaps package data with deterministic normalized GeoJSON, immutable provenance/checksum manifests, shipped license and attribution files, importlib.resources loading, offline installed-wheel coverage, and an enforced main-wheel-at-or-below-60-MiB or version-matched anima-ai-data companion-wheel outcome.

A deterministic MapDataset registry and territory index mapping canonical dataset-scoped IDs to valid Shapely geometry and half-open date-validity intervals, with unique-ID, alias, non-overlapping-validity, stable-serialization, direct lookup, enumeration, and historical date-resolution coverage.

Pinned-pyproj projection support with EPSG:8857 Equal Earth as the world default and caller-centered WGS84 Lambert azimuthal equal-area as the regional default, canonical serializable definitions, always_xy axis handling, PROJ networking disabled, geometry-type preservation, and deterministic forward/inverse round-trip tests.

Topology-aware collection simplification using shared quantized topojson arcs, with explicit deterministic parameters and regression fixtures proving valid adjacent-country geometries retain coincident borders while introducing neither measurable interior overlaps nor coverage gaps across multiple tolerances.
